title: python-assignments-glr-merge
description: Python/Assignments failure: 'a, *b.c = d' produces attribute(list_splat(b), c) instead of list_splat_pattern(attribute(b, c)). CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE (2026-02-24): NOT a GLR merge disambiguation issue — nodeAddLink Case 1/2 and condenseStack merge never fire for this test. The actual root cause is reduce/reduce conflict ordering in advanceVersion: list_splat (sym=149) vs list_splat_pattern (sym=184). In C, the last reduce becomes primary via renumber. Go's swap-based approach selects the wrong primary. Fix: coder-1's advanceVersion flat loop restructure (Split+doReduce+renumber matching C's linear loop) will resolve this.
